agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. Enforces organizational policy on advance notice, manager approval workflows, and year-end forfeiture rules.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued leave days available to an employee, calculated as total accrued minus total approved-and-taken leave."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which an employee earns 1.5 leave days for each month worked."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to take leave for a specified date range, subject to balance availability, advance notice, and manager approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (within 5 days of the absence) when accompanied by a doctor's note, exempt from the standard advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days as defined by the company calendar, excluding weekends and public holidays, used to calculate advance notice periods."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's current employment contract terminates; leave requests must not extend beyond this boundary."},{"term":"Carry-Over","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where unused leave days (up to a maximum of 10) are transferred to the next year's balance."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The automatic loss of unused leave days exceeding the 10-day carry-over cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate from a licensed practitioner, required as supporting documentation for retroactive medical leave requests."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave for each full month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust (e.g., to 2.0 days/month for senior employees). The mechanism of accrual is invariant, but the rate is policy.","testable_assertion":"Given an employee who has worked N complete months, their total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An employee's leave balance must never go below zero. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative balance represents consuming a resource that does not exist — this is a hard data integrity constraint. Allowing it would corrupt the leave ledger.","testable_assertion":"For any employee at any point in time: leave_balance >= 0, and no approved leave request causes the balance to drop below zero."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A non-medical leave request must be submitted at least 3 working days before the leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-day window is an operational policy that management could relax or tighten. The system must enforce whatever the configured threshold is, but the threshold itself is policy.","testable_assertion":"Given a non-medical leave request, the number of working days between submission date and leave start date must be >= 3, otherwise the request is rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave may be submitted retroactively within 5 days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day window and documentation requirement are HR policy, not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a medical leave request submitted after the leave dates, the submission date must be within 5 days of the last absence day AND a doctor's note must be attached; otherwise the request is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only a manager can approve or reject a leave request. The employee cannot self-approve.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Separation of duties between requester and approver is a fundamental control. Violating it undermines the entire approval workflow's integrity.","testable_assertion":"The actor who approves/rejects a leave request must have the Manager role and must not be the requesting employee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave requests do not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Consuming balance on a rejected request would corrupt the leave ledger — the employee never took the leave, so no balance should be deducted.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request transitions to REJECTED status, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on approved leave when they are no longer employed. This is a legal/contractual integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"For any leave request, every requested leave day must fall on or before the employee's contract end date."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave days exceeding 10 are forfeited. A maximum of 10 unused days may carry over to the next calendar year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day cap is a configurable HR policy. The forfeiture mechanism itself is invariant, but the threshold is not.","testable_assertion":"On January 1 of each year, an employee's carried-over balance equals min(previous_year_unused_balance, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 complete months and has not taken any leave","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient balance' and balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request on a Monday","when":"The requested leave start date is the immediately following Wednesday (2 working days away)","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient advance notice'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request on a Monday","when":"The requested leave start date is the following Thursday (3 working days away)","then":"The request passes the advance notice validation"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent on March 10 due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request on March 14 (4 days later) with a doctor's note attached","then":"The request passes the retroactive submission validation"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent on March 10 due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request on March 16 (6 days later) with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'retroactive medical leave submitted beyond 5-day window'"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'doctor's note required for retroactive medical leave'"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A leave request is in PENDING status","when":"The employee's manager approves it","then":"The request transitions to APPROVED and the employee's balance is reduced by the number of leave days"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A leave request is in PENDING status","when":"The employee's manager rejects it","then":"The request transitions to REJECTED and the employee's balance is unchanged"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on June 30","when":"They submit a leave request for June 28 to July 2","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'leave request extends beyond contract end date'"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The calendar year rolls over to January 1","then":"Their new balance is 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The calendar year rolls over to January 1","then":"Their new balance is 8 days and 0 days are forfeited"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee starts mid-month — does a partial month count toward accrual?","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous in spec. System must define whether partial months accrue prorated leave (e.g., 15 days worked in a 30-day month = 0.75 days) or require a full month."},{"scenario":"Leave request submitted exactly on the boundary of the 5-day retroactive medical window","expected_behavior":"The 5th day should be inclusive — a request submitted on exactly day 5 after the absence is still valid."},{"scenario":"Employee submits leave for a date range that includes weekends/holidays — do non-working days consume balance?","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous. Typically only working days within the range consume balance, but the spec does not clarify."},{"scenario":"Manager is on leave themselves — who approves requests?","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address delegation of approval authority. System likely needs a delegate/escalation mechanism."},{"scenario":"Two overlapping leave requests for the same dates submitted simultaneously","expected_behavior":"System should prevent double-booking the same dates. If one is approved, the second should be rejected or flagged as conflicting."},{"scenario":"Employee's contract end date is updated after leave was already approved beyond the new end date","expected_behavior":"Previously approved leave that now extends beyond the contract end date should be flagged for review or automatically revoked."},{"scenario":"Medical leave submitted retroactively but the absence spans multiple days — does the 5-day window start from the first or last day of absence?","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous. Recommendation: measure from the last day of absence, as the employee may still be ill during multi-day absences."},{"scenario":"Year-end carry-over when employee has approved future leave that straddles Dec 31 / Jan 1","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous. Must decide whether the carry-over calculation considers leave already approved but not yet taken."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or carry-over logic can lead to over-granting leave (financial liability from excess paid time off) or under-granting (employee disputes, potential legal claims). Forfeiture errors in either direction have direct payroll implications.","operational":"Leave balance accuracy directly affects workforce planning. Managers depend on correct balances to ensure adequate staffing. Faulty advance-notice rules undermine the ability to plan coverage.","user":"Employees expect transparent, predictable leave balances. Rejected requests due to system errors erode trust. Medical leave flexibility is critical for employee wellbeing and retention.","risk":"Non-negative balance invariant violation is the highest risk — it could allow unlimited leave consumption. Contract end date enforcement has legal compliance implications. Carry-over forfeiture errors could trigger labor disputes.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct consumers of leave accrual, requests, and carry-over. Errors affect their earned benefits and time-off planning."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approval workflow. Need accurate balance data for staffing decisions. Lack of delegation rules may cause approval bottlenecks."},{"group":"HR / Payroll","impact":"Depend on accurate leave ledger for payroll processing, compliance reporting, and policy enforcement. Forfeiture errors create reconciliation burden."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Leave liabilities appear on the balance sheet. Inaccurate accrual or carry-over affects financial reporting."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Contract end date enforcement and leave entitlement accuracy have regulatory implications depending on jurisdiction."}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The core rules are clearly stated and internally consistent — no contradictions detected. However, several operationally important details are left ambiguous: partial month accrual, whether the 5-day medical window uses calendar or working days, how non-working days within a leave range are counted, and the precise timing of year-end carry-over processing. These ambiguities would lead to divergent implementations.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Manager approval/rejection workflow","Rejected leave does not consume balance","Contract end date boundary enforcement","Carry-over cap of 10 days with forfeiture"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial month accrual calculation","Whether '5 days' for medical retroactive submission means calendar or working days","Whether leave days within a request count only working days or all calendar days","Approval delegation when manager is unavailable","Year-end carry-over timing relative to in-flight approved leave"],"assumptions":["The 5-day retroactive medical window is measured in calendar days from the last day of absence","Only working days within a leave request range consume balance","Accrual of 1.5 days applies to complete months only; partial months do not accrue","Year-end carry-over is calculated on December 31 after all leave for the year is settled"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial month accrual: the spec says '1.5 days per month worked' but does not define whether a partial month (e.g., employee starts on the 15th) earns prorated accrual or nothing.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR / Payroll"],"options":["No accrual for partial months — only complete calendar months count","Prorated accrual based on days worked in the partial month","Accrual starts from the first full month after hire date"],"recommendation":"Prorated accrual is most equitable and standard in HR systems, but HR policy owners must confirm."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The '5 days' window for retroactive medical leave submission does not specify calendar days vs working days. For a Friday absence, 5 calendar days = Wednesday vs 5 working days = the following Friday.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR / Payroll"],"options":["5 calendar days from last day of absence","5 working days from last day of absence"],"recommendation":"Calendar days is simpler and more common for medical contexts (illness does not respect business calendars). Recommend calendar days."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not clarify whether leave days within a request consume only working days or all calendar days in the range. A Mon-Fri request spanning a weekend: is it 5 days or 7?","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","Managers","HR / Payroll"],"options":["Only working days (per company calendar) within the range consume balance","All calendar days in the range consume balance"],"recommendation":"Working days only is the industry standard for leave management. Recommend working days."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Year-end carry-over timing is unspecified. If an employee has 14 unused days but 3 days of approved leave starting Dec 29, the balance on Dec 31 depends on whether in-flight leave is deducted before or after carry-over calculation.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","Finance","HR / Payroll"],"options":["Carry-over calculated after all approved leave for the year is deducted","Carry-over calculated based on balance at midnight Dec 31 regardless of in-flight leave"],"recommendation":"Calculate carry-over based on the effective balance after all leave consumed in the calendar year, to avoid penalizing employees for year-end leave."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

Enforces organizational policy on advance notice, manager approval workflows, and year-end forfeiture rules.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued leave days available to an employee, calculated as total accrued minus total approved-and-tak

content.thinking:
Clear HR leave management domain. Let me analyze this thoroughly.

The rules are well-defined. Let me check for any ambiguities or issues.

Key rules:
1. Accrual: 1.5 days/month
2. No negative balance
3. 3 working days advance notice (except medical)
4. Medical leave: retroactive within 5 days with doctor's note
5. Manager approval/rejection
6. Rejected leave doesn't consume balance
7. Cannot span contract end dates
8. Carry-over cap: 10 days, excess forfeited

Let me think about ambiguities:


- "per month worked" — what about partial months? Start mid-month?
- "3 working days" — whose working days? Company calendar?
- "5 days" for medical — working days or calendar days?
- "next calendar year" — when exactly does forfeiture happen?
- Carry-over cap of 10 days — is this checked at year-end or continuously?

These are ambiguities — the spec uses vague terms that could be interpreted multiple ways. The primary blocker type is `ambiguity`. Confidence is at most 55 due to these unresolved ambiguities.

The system must enforce whatever the configured threshold is, but the threshold itself is policy.","testable_assertion":"Given a non-medical leave request, the number of working days between submission date and leave start date must be >= 3, otherwise the request is rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave may be submitted retroactively within 5 days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day window and documentation requirement are HR policy, not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a medical leave request submitted after the leave dates, the submission date must be within 5 days of the last absence day AND a doctor's note must be attached; otherwise the request is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only a manager can approve or reject a leave request. The employee cannot self-approve.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Separation of duties between requester and approver is a fundamental control. Violating it undermines the entire approval workflow's integrity.","testable_assertion":"The actor who approves/rejects a leave request must have the Manager role and must not be the requesting employee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave requests do not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Consuming balance on a rejected request would corrupt the leave ledger — the employee never took the leave, so no balance should be deducted.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request transitions to REJECTED status, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on approved leave when they are no longer employed. This is a legal/contractual integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"For any leave request, every requested leave day must fall on or before the employee's contract end date."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave days exceeding 10 are forfeited. A maximum of 10 unused days may carry over to the next calendar year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day cap is a configurable HR policy. The forfeiture mechanism itself is invariant, but the threshold is not.","testable_assertion":"On January 1 of each year, an employee's carried-over balance equals min(previous_year_unused_balance,
